roles.14386: triggering alert: certutil.exe -privatekey -exportpfx 'Computer' Computer.pfx — exports the machine certificate's PRIVATE KEY to a PFX (T1552.004 credential access)
roles.14673: repeat: certutil.exe -privatekey -exportpfx '1234' test.pfx — a second private-key export
roles.20445: certutil -Store MY — enumerates the personal certificate store
roles.20623: certutil -exportPFX My <thumbprint> C:\cert.pfx — exports a specific certificate by thumbprint
roles.14745: certutil -dcinfo — queries domain controller certificate info

metadata.security_proposition: The observed private-key exports are malicious credential theft rather than legitimate certificate administration.

attack_category.category: Credential access via certificate private-key export (unverified intent)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = WEAK. A High-integrity session enumerates the cert store (-Store MY, -dcinfo) and exports certificate PRIVATE KEYS to PFX (-privatekey -exportpfx). Exporting a private key is a real T1552.004 indicator, but intent is AMBIGUOUS — certutil is a legitimate Microsoft administration utility and certificate export/backup can be a normal task, while there is NO evidence the exported keys were exfiltrated or used. The package does not verify a digital signature. Role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): the model must not conclude 'confirmed private-key theft'. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: all certutil operations are captured in this host's windows-sysmon.log (EID1); single-sensor Tier-1.

boundary_definition: A0 = the -privatekey -exportpfx of the machine ('Computer') certificate — the most sensitive export.
EV = the surrounding cert operations (a second private-key export, store enumeration, a thumbprint export, DC cert info).
EID1 (process_create) ONLY — the certutil command lines are the evidence; no follow-on exfiltration or use of the exported PFX is captured.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: certutil.exe -privatekey -exportpfx (exporting a certificate private key) at High integrity is the T1552.004 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: Exporting a machine-certificate private key is the most sensitive operation in the session; intent is unverified.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: WEAK / over-triage-prone. Private-key export is a genuine T1552.004 indicator, but certutil is a legitimate administration utility and certificate backup can be legitimate; NO exfiltration or misuse is captured. The package does not verify a digital signature. The model must not jump to 'confirmed credential theft -> High'.
No network or file event shows the exported PFX leaving the host or being used; the malicious OUTCOME is uncorroborated.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
